Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and bilirubinuria, suggesting possible renal dysfunction and impaired filtration. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, and contains glucose and ketones, raising concern for tubular damage or early diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. No evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of renal impairment.
